blocks: The bottom layer has a fixed zero baseline. Its top boundary is position along a common scale. The reader can compare Fo high.
The second layer has a variable baseline — the top of the bottom layer, which moves. The reader is comparing second-laye lower.

S08.meta: // SLIDE 08 — HOW THIS CHANGES THE PROMPT

data.title: HOW THIS CHANGES THE PROMPT.
blocks: · Continuous x-axis with no skipped intervals (or an explicit gap marker if data is missing).
· Time running left to right.
· Axis tick labels at meaningful time boundaries (year starts, not arbitrary month offsets).
For multi-series either direct labels at line endpoints or a legend positioned so the reader's eye does not have to travel far from the r

data.title: EXERCISES
blocks: · Daily website visits over a year, single series, the question is trend.
· Monthly humanitarian funding across five sectors, the question is composition and total.
· Hourly energy consumption over a week, the question is the daily cycle.
· Project tasks with start dates, durations, and dependencies for a six-month initiative.
· Three data points (2010, 2015, 2020) for a social indicator, question is whether change was consistent.

Spiral plot.
